image.alt: Handcrafted Khatam jewelry box showing detailed marquetry work

hero.headline: Hands That Create.
hero.summary: A family-led traditional craft workshop in İzmit, working to revive the art of Khatam and create sustainable training opportunities for vulnerable communities.
story: We are a family of five who arrived in Türkiye as refugees 12 years ago and settled in İzmit.
We brought no resources with us — only our skills, our faith in work, and a traditional craft rooted in centuries of history: Khatam art.
Over the years, we built a life focused on community support and service to displaced families, while quietly working to keep this fragile craft alive.

pillars.title: The Three Pillars of Our Work
pillars.description: Our vision is to combine traditional craftsmanship with modern digital tools to create a sustainable creative ecosystem.

items.title: Preservation
items.description: Keeping endangered traditional craftsmanship techniques alive.

items.title: Empowerment
items.description: Providing training and creating employment opportunities for refugee communities.

items.title: Global Reach
items.description: Sharing authentic handmade work with wider audiences through digital visibility.

vision.title: What We Are Working Toward
paragraphs: We are working toward building a focused and scalable craft initiative. Our objectives include training a small group of artisans each year, setting up a reliable workshop, and developing a production process that ensures quality and consistency. At the same time, we aim to expand our reach through digital platforms, making our products accessible to a global audience.

Our mission is to revive the art of Khatam by passing its knowledge to a new generation of makers. We focus on supporting individuals from underserved communities, helping them develop skills that lead to dignity, independence, and creative expression. Through this journey, we aim to protect cultural heritage while building real opportunities for people.

items.label: Phase 03 (Months 5–6)
items.title: Sharing
steps: Begin sharing skills with a very small group of learners
Document the process for transparency and education

items.label: Phase 04 (6+ Months)
items.title: Continuity
steps: Expand only if stability is achieved

experience.title: What We Bring Into This Work
experience.intro: This project is built on years of lived practice and real-world experience:
experience.quote: We do not present ourselves as a large institution, but as a small family continuing a craft with the tools we have learned over time.
items: Over a decade of life and service in Türkiye as a family
Long-term practice in traditional Khatam craftsmanship
Experience documenting our craft process through video content
A previously active online presence where we shared our work journey
Practical understanding of digital tools such as website creation and UX/UI design
Familiarity with online platforms and content-based outreach

portfolioPreview.title: Our Work
portfolioPreview.description: A selection of handcrafted pieces created during different stages of our journey.

reality.title: Why the Art
reality.description: We have not stopped; we are preserving our energy until the moment we have the structural support to rebuild.

items.title: Workshop Space
items.description: Khatam-kari requires a stable, dust-free environment for precise gluing and cutting. Currently, we work from a limited living space.

items.title: Precision Tools
items.description: The craft depends on specialized saws and presses. Without them, we can only produce small-scale items.

items.title: Raw Materials
items.description: Sourcing high-quality brass, bone, and wood requires upfront capital that is currently prioritized for survival.

currentSituation.title: Where We
currentSituation.description: At this stage, our work continues in a very limited and unstable environment.
points: We do not have a dedicated workshop space
We have limited tools and equipment
We are not currently producing at a stable or consistent level
Most of our time is divided between daily work and sustaining life needs
currentSituation.quote: Despite this, we continue to protect the craft in a small and informal way whenever possible.

whyContinue.title: Why We
paragraphs: Khatam is a delicate and time-intensive craft that requires patience and precision. It is part of a broader cultural heritage that is slowly disappearing as fewer people continue the practice.
At the same time, we have seen how learning a craft can bring structure, dignity, and focus to individuals who are rebuilding their lives in a new place.
whyContinue.quote: For us, this work is not only production. It is memory, identity, and continuity.

supportNeeds.title: What We Are
supportNeeds.quote: we are asking for a chance to rebuild a stable foundation for craft, learning, and production.

items.title: Workshop Space
items.description: A small, dedicated environment to restart stable and professional production.

items.title: Tools & Materials
items.description: The basic equipment and high-quality raw materials required for traditional Khatam craftsmanship.

items.title: Foundational Support
items.description: Support, grant, or sponsorship to cover initial setup costs and ensure continuity during the transition.

transparency.title: Commitment to
transparency.description: We understand that trust is built through clarity. It is our responsibility to show exactly how your support translates into real-world craft preservation.

items.title: Sharing updates on progress
items.description: Regular milestones shared with our supporters.

items.title: Documenting workshop development
items.description: Step-by-step visual reports on the new space.

items.title: Showing real production and process
items.description: Behind-the-scenes views of the craftsmanship and the daily work.

items.title: Financial transparency
items.description: Clear reporting on how every grant or support contribution is used.

finalCta.title: Help Us Rebuild a Small Space
finalCta.description: With a stable workspace, this craft can continue to exist and slowly be shared with others who may benefit from learning it.
